## ὀργή (id: 003616000000000)

ὀργή
====

ὀργή (G3709)

noun, f. \| \-ῆς \| ὀργίζομαι

88\.173 · Moral and Ethical Qualities and Related Behavior · Anger, Be Indignant With

anger, fury a relative state of anger In a number of languages it is impossible to speak of ‘anger’ without indicating against whom the anger exists. For example, in [1TI 2:8](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:8) it might be possible to say ‘to pray without anger against anyone’ or ‘to pray without being angry at anyone.’

[MRK 3:5](https://ref.ly/Matt3:5) · [ROM 1:18](https://ref.ly/Acts1:18) · [ROM 2:8](https://ref.ly/Acts2:8) · [ROM 9:22](https://ref.ly/Acts9:22) · [ROM 9:22](https://ref.ly/Acts9:22) · [EPH 4:31](https://ref.ly/Gal4:31) · [COL 3:8](https://ref.ly/Phil3:8) · [1TH 1:10](https://ref.ly/Col1:10) · [1TH 2:16](https://ref.ly/Col2:16) · [1TH 5:9](https://ref.ly/Col5:9) · [1TI 2:8](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:8) · [HEB 3:11](https://ref.ly/Phlm3:11) · [HEB 4:3](https://ref.ly/Phlm4:3) · [JAS 1:19](https://ref.ly/Heb1:19) · [JAS 1:20](https://ref.ly/Heb1:20) · [REV 6:16](https://ref.ly/Jude6:16) · [REV 6:17](https://ref.ly/Jude6:17) · [REV 11:18](https://ref.ly/Jude11:18) · [REV 14:10](https://ref.ly/Jude14:10) · [REV 16:19](https://ref.ly/Jude16:19) · [REV 19:15](https://ref.ly/Jude19:15)

38\.10 · Punish, Reward · Punish

to punish, punishment divine punishment based on God’s angry judgment against someone Though the focal semantic element in ὀργή\[b] is punishment, at the same time there is an implication of God’s anger because of evil. Therefore, it is possible in some languages to translate this expression in [ROM 3:5](https://ref.ly/Acts3:5) as ‘God does not do wrong when he is angry and punishes us, does he?’

[MAT 3:7](https://ref.ly/Matt3:7) · [LUK 3:7](https://ref.ly/Mark3:7) · [LUK 21:23](https://ref.ly/Mark21:23) · [JHN 3:36](https://ref.ly/Luke3:36) · [ROM 2:5](https://ref.ly/Acts2:5) · [ROM 2:5](https://ref.ly/Acts2:5) · [ROM 3:5](https://ref.ly/Acts3:5) · [ROM 4:15](https://ref.ly/Acts4:15) · [ROM 5:9](https://ref.ly/Acts5:9) · [ROM 12:19](https://ref.ly/Acts12:19) · [ROM 13:4](https://ref.ly/Acts13:4) · [ROM 13:5](https://ref.ly/Acts13:5) · [EPH 2:3](https://ref.ly/Gal2:3) · [EPH 5:6](https://ref.ly/Gal5:6) · [COL 3:6](https://ref.ly/Phil3:6) · [1TH 1:10](https://ref.ly/Col1:10) · [1TH 2:16](https://ref.ly/Col2:16) · [1TH 5:9](https://ref.ly/Col5:9)

* **Associated Passages:** Mark 3:5; Romans 1:18; Romans 2:8; Romans 9:22; Ephesians 4:31; Colossians 3:8; 1 Thessalonians 1:10; 1 Thessalonians 2:16; 1 Thessalonians 5:9; 1 Timothy 2:8; Hebrews 3:11; Hebrews 4:3; James 1:19; James 1:20; Revelation 6:16; Revelation 6:17; Revelation 11:18; Revelation 14:10; Revelation 16:19; Revelation 19:15; Matthew 3:7; Luke 3:7; Luke 21:23; John 3:36; Romans 2:5; Romans 3:5; Romans 4:15; Romans 5:9; Romans 12:19; Romans 13:4; Romans 13:5; Ephesians 2:3; Ephesians 5:6; Colossians 3:6


## ὀργίζω (id: 003617000000000)

ὀργίζω
======

ὀργίζω (G3710) — ὀργίζομαι

verb \| ὀργή · ὀργίλος · παροργίζω · παροργισμός · προορίζω

88\.174 · (ὀργίζομαι) · Moral and Ethical Qualities and Related Behavior · Anger, Be Indignant With

to be full of anger, to be furious, to be angry to be relatively angry

[MAT 5:22](https://ref.ly/Matt5:22) · [MAT 18:34](https://ref.ly/Matt18:34) · [MAT 22:7](https://ref.ly/Matt22:7) · [LUK 14:21](https://ref.ly/Mark14:21) · [LUK 15:28](https://ref.ly/Mark15:28) · [EPH 4:26](https://ref.ly/Gal4:26) · [REV 11:18](https://ref.ly/Jude11:18) · [REV 12:17](https://ref.ly/Jude12:17)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 5:22; Matthew 18:34; Matthew 22:7; Luke 14:21; Luke 15:28; Ephesians 4:26; Revelation 11:18; Revelation 12:17


## ὀργίλος (id: 003618000000000)

ὀργίλος
=======

ὀργίλος (G3711)

adjc. \| \-η, \-ον \| ὀργίζομαι

88\.175 · Moral and Ethical Qualities and Related Behavior · Anger, Be Indignant With

angry, quick\-tempered, given to anger pertaining to a tendency to become angry

[TIT 1:7](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:7)

* **Associated Passages:** Titus 1:7


## ὀργυιά (id: 003619000000000)

ὀργυιά
======

ὀργυιά (G3712)

noun, f. \| \-ᾶς \| ὀρέγομαι

81\.26 · Spacial Dimensions · Specific Measures of Length

fathom traditionally the measurement of a man’s arms stretched out horizontally, reckoned at approximately six feet or almost two meters and used as a technical, nautical term to measure the depth of water If one adopts a metric system, the later expression in [ACT 27:28](https://ref.ly/John27:28), namely, ὀργυιὰς δεκαπέντε ‘fifteen fathoms,’ may be rendered as ‘about thirty meters.’

[ACT 27:28](https://ref.ly/John27:28) · [ACT 27:28](https://ref.ly/John27:28)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 27:28


## ὀρέγω (id: 003620000000000)

ὀρέγω
=====

ὀρέγω (G3713) — ὀρέγομαι

verb \| ὀργυιά · ὄρεξις

25\.15 · (ὀρέγομαι) · Attitudes and Emotions · Desire Strongly

to strive to attain, to aspire to, to eagerly long for to eagerly desire to accomplish some goal or purpose1

[1TI 3:1](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:1) · [1TI 6:10](https://ref.ly/2Thess6:10) · [HEB 11:16](https://ref.ly/Phlm11:16)

1 The meaning of ὀρέγομαι normally involves greater intensity of feeling than the meaning of ἐπιθυμέω\[a] and ἐπιθυμία\[a] ([25\.12](#25.12)).

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Timothy 3:1; 1 Timothy 6:10; Hebrews 11:16


## ὀρεινός (id: 003621000000000)

ὀρεινός
=======

ὀρεινός (G3714) — ὀρεινή

adjc. \| \-ή, \-όν

1\.47 · Geographical Objects and Features · Elevated Land Formations

mountainous region, hill country pertaining to an area characterized by mountains or hills

[LUK 1:39](https://ref.ly/Mark1:39) · [LUK 1:65](https://ref.ly/Mark1:65)

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 1:39; Luke 1:65


## ὄρεξις (id: 003622000000000)

ὄρεξις
======

ὄρεξις (G3715)

noun, f. \| \-εως \| ὀρέγομαι

25\.16 · Attitudes and Emotions · Desire Strongly

**Collocation:** ἐκκαίομαι ἐν τῇ ὀρέξει

to be inflamed with passion, to have a strong lust for, to be inflamed with lust (an idiom, literally: to burn with intense desire) to have a strong, intense desire for something In some languages the equivalent idiom is ‘to boil with desire,’ ‘to feel hot in the genitals,’ or ‘to prefer to die rather than to do.’

[ROM 1:27](https://ref.ly/Acts1:27)

* **Associated Passages:** Romans 1:27


## ὀρθοποδέω (id: 003623000000000)

ὀρθοποδέω
=========

ὀρθοποδέω (G3716)

verb \| ὀρθός \+ πούς

41\.36 · Behavior and Related States · Particular Patterns of Behavior

to live right, to live as one ought to to live a life of moral correctness ὀρθοποδέω may be translated in a number of languages as ‘to live as one should.’

[GAL 2:14](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:14)

* **Associated Passages:** Galatians 2:14


## ὀρθός (id: 003624000000000)

ὀρθός
=====

ὀρθός (G3717)

adjc. \| \-ή, \-όν \| ἀνορθόω · διόρθωμα · διόρθωσις · ἐπανόρθωσις · ἐπιδιορθόω · ὀρθοποδέω · ὀρθοτομέω · ὀρθῶς

79\.88 · Features of Objects · Straight, Crooked

straight, direct pertaining to being straight in contrast to what is crooked1 The context of [HEB 12:13](https://ref.ly/Phlm12:13) is, of course, figurative.

[ACT 14:10](https://ref.ly/John14:10) · [HEB 12:13](https://ref.ly/Phlm12:13)

41\.30 · Behavior and Related States · Particular Patterns of Behavior

**Collocation:** τροχιὰς ὀρθὰς ποιέω τοῖς ποσίν

to live, to behave, to conduct oneself correctly (an idiom, literally: to make straight wheel tracks for the feet) to live or behave in strict conformance to a predetermined model for behavior

[HEB 12:13](https://ref.ly/Phlm12:13)

1 In earlier Greek ὀρθός meant straight up and down while εὐθύς meant straight on the horizontal plane, but this distinction has not always been preserved in Koine Greek.

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 14:10; Hebrews 12:13


## ὀρθοτομέω (id: 003625000000000)

ὀρθοτομέω
=========

to teach correctly, to expound rightly to give accurate instruction

## ὄρθρος (id: 003628000000000)

ὄρθρος
======

ὄρθρος (G3722)

noun, m. \| \-ου \| ὀρθρίζω

67\.187

early morning the early part of the daylight period

[JHN 8:2](https://ref.ly/Luke8:2)

67\.73 · Time · A Point of Time with Reference to Units of Time: Daybreak, Midday, Midnight, Late

daybreak, sunrise, dawn the point of time when the day begins1

[LUK 24:1](https://ref.ly/Mark24:1) · [ACT 5:21](https://ref.ly/John5:21)

1 There may be slight differences of meaning between αὐγή and ὄρθρος\[b] in that αὐγή focuses primarily upon light and ὄρθρος\[b] perhaps on the early part of dawn, but this cannot be determined precisely from existing contexts.

* **Associated Passages:** John 8:2; Luke 24:1; Acts 5:21

## ὅριον (id: 003631000000000)

ὅριον
=====

ὅριον (G3725)

noun, n. \| \-ου \| ὁρίζω

1\.79 · Geographical Objects and Features · Sociopolitical Areas

region, territory, land, district (though this might imply too precisely a governmental area) region or regions of the earth, normally in relation to some ethnic group or geographical center, but not necessarily constituting a unit of governmental administration1 The meanings of γῆ\[d], χώρα\[b], κλίμαὅριον, and μέρος\[c] are not to be regarded as completely synonymous, but in certain of their NT usages they are largely overlapping in reference.

[MAT 2:16](https://ref.ly/Matt2:16) · [MAT 4:13](https://ref.ly/Matt4:13) · [MAT 8:34](https://ref.ly/Matt8:34) · [MAT 15:22](https://ref.ly/Matt15:22) · [MAT 15:39](https://ref.ly/Matt15:39) · [MAT 19:1](https://ref.ly/Matt19:1) · [MRK 5:17](https://ref.ly/Matt5:17) · [MRK 7:24](https://ref.ly/Matt7:24) · [MRK 7:31](https://ref.ly/Matt7:31) · [MRK 7:31](https://ref.ly/Matt7:31) · [MRK 10:1](https://ref.ly/Matt10:1) · [ACT 13:50](https://ref.ly/John13:50)

1 The overlapping features of meaning in γῆ\[d], χώρα\[b], κλίμα, ὅριον, and μέρος\[c] may be readily illustrated by the comparable overlapping of English terms such as region, territory, and land. For example, in the case of [MAT 15:21](https://ref.ly/Matt15:21) it would be possible to speak of the region of Tyre and Sidon, the territory of Tyre and Sidon, the land of Tyre and Sidon, and even the district of Tyre and Sidon. The English terms region, territory, land, and district do differ in certain nuances of meaning reflecting primarily supplementary components which are largely connotative.

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 2:16; Matthew 4:13; Matthew 8:34; Matthew 15:22; Matthew 15:39; Matthew 19:1; Mark 5:17; Mark 7:24; Mark 7:31; Mark 10:1; Acts 13:50


## ὁρκίζω (id: 003632000000000)

ὁρκίζω
======

ὁρκίζω (G3726)

verb \| ἐνορκίζω · ἐξορκίζω · ἐξορκιστής · ἐπιορκέω · ἐπίορκος · ὅρκος · ὁρκωμοσία

33\.467 · Communication · Swear, Put Under Oath, Vow

to put under oath, to insist that one take an oath, to require that one swear to demand that a person take an oath as to the truth of what is said or as to the certainty that one will carry out the request or command1 It is extremely difficult to translate ὁρκίζω, ἐνορκίζω, and ἐξορκίζω in a literal manner, though in some contexts one can say ‘I put you under oath,’ but in a number of passages the person speaking is imploring and not necessarily in a position to command or insist. Therefore, in a passage such as [MRK 5:7](https://ref.ly/Matt5:7), one may render the meaning idiomatically as ‘for God’s sake, I ask you, do not punish me.’ In [1TH 5:27](https://ref.ly/Col5:27) one may translate ‘in the name of the Lord, I ask you to read this letter to all the Christian brothers,’ and in [MAT 26:63](https://ref.ly/Matt26:63) one may translate ‘in the name of the living God, I charge you, Tell us who you are.’  
In most languages one may translate ὁρκίζω, ἐνορκίζω, and ἐξορκίζω by simply adding a causative component to terms meaning ‘to swear’ or ‘to take an oath.’ In other words, ὁρκίζω, ἐνορκίζω, and ἐξορκίζω may be rendered as ‘to cause a person to say under oath.’

[MRK 5:7](https://ref.ly/Matt5:7) · [ACT 19:13](https://ref.ly/John19:13)

1 It is possible that ὁρκίζω, ἐνορκίζω, and ἐξορκίζω differ slightly in meaning, but this cannot be determined on the basis of available contexts.

* **Associated Passages:** Mark 5:7; Acts 19:13


## ὅρκος (id: 003633000000000)

ὅρκος
=====

ὅρκος (G3727)

noun, m. \| \-ου \| ὁρκίζω

33\.463 · Communication · Swear, Put Under Oath, Vow

to swear, to make an oath, oath to affirm the truth of a statement by calling on a divine being to execute sanctions against a person if the statement in question is not true (in the case of a deity taking an oath, his divine being is regarded as validating the statement) In a number of languages it is necessary to be quite specific in referring to the swearing of an oath, for example, ‘to say something by calling upon God to listen’ or ‘to state that something is true and asking God to punish if it is not true’ or ‘to make God responsible for what one has said.’

[MAT 5:33](https://ref.ly/Matt5:33) · [MAT 14:7](https://ref.ly/Matt14:7) · [MAT 14:9](https://ref.ly/Matt14:9) · [MAT 26:72](https://ref.ly/Matt26:72) · [MRK 6:26](https://ref.ly/Matt6:26) · [LUK 1:73](https://ref.ly/Mark1:73) · [ACT 2:30](https://ref.ly/John2:30) · [HEB 6:16](https://ref.ly/Phlm6:16) · [HEB 6:17](https://ref.ly/Phlm6:17) · [JAS 5:12](https://ref.ly/Heb5:12)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 5:33; Matthew 14:7; Matthew 14:9; Matthew 26:72; Mark 6:26; Luke 1:73; Acts 2:30; Hebrews 6:16; Hebrews 6:17; James 5:12

## ὄρνεον (id: 003638000000000)

ὄρνεον
======

ὄρνεον (G3732)

noun, n. \| \-ου \| ὄρνις

4\.38 · Animals · Birds

bird any kind of bird, wild or domestic All languages have at least some terms for birds, but there may be no generic term which identifies birds while excluding all other creatures which fly. For example, in some languages a term which includes all kinds of birds also includes flying insects and bats. However, since in most instances such a term has birds as its primary reference, it can usually be employed in contexts in which birds are more or less readily identified by other expressions in the passage. In other languages generic terms for birds may identify only certain classes of birds, for example, birds of prey, water birds, and birds of the forest.  
In the NT occurrences of ὄρνεον, the reference is to unclean birds, either birds of prey or scavengers.

[REV 18:2](https://ref.ly/Jude18:2) · [REV 19:17](https://ref.ly/Jude19:17) · [REV 19:21](https://ref.ly/Jude19:21)

* **Associated Passages:** Revelation 18:2; Revelation 19:17; Revelation 19:21


## ὄρνις (id: 003639000000000)

ὄρνις
=====

ὄρνις (G3733)

noun, f. \| \-ίθος \| ὄρνεον

4\.38 · Animals · Birds

bird any kind of bird, wild or domestic All languages have at least some terms for birds, but there may be no generic term which identifies birds while excluding all other creatures which fly. For example, in some languages a term which includes all kinds of birds also includes flying insects and bats. However, since in most instances such a term has birds as its primary reference, it can usually be employed in contexts in which birds are more or less readily identified by other expressions in the passage. In other languages generic terms for birds may identify only certain classes of birds, for example, birds of prey, water birds, and birds of the forest.  
In this only NT occurrence (with parallel), ὄρνις probably designates a hen.

[MAT 23:37](https://ref.ly/Matt23:37) · [LUK 13:34](https://ref.ly/Mark13:34)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 23:37; Luke 13:34

## ὄρος (id: 003641000000000)

ὄρος
====

ὄρος (G3735)

noun, n. \| \-ους

1\.46 · Geographical Objects and Features · Elevated Land Formations

mountain a relatively high elevation of land, in contrast with βουνός hill ([1\.48](#1.48)), which is by comparison somewhat lower Terms referring to mountains, hills, or mounds inevitably reflect the nature of the surrounding landscape. For example, in the flat marshlands of the Central Sudan, an elevation called Doleib Hill is only some three or four feet above the surrounding area, but in the Himalayas elevations of land some ten or twelve thousand feet are still regarded as hills in contrast with the mountains. In the choice of receptor language terms for ὄρος and βουνός ([1\.48](#1.48)), one must seek expressions which suggest corresponding differences. What is important is relative size, not an exact correspondence to biblical geography. It may, however, be necessary in some instances to introduce a footnote to describe the approximate height of certain biblical mountains or hills.

=======

orphan an offspring whose parents either are no longer alive or no longer function as parents (as the result of having abandoned their offspring) Some languages make a distinction in terms for ‘orphan’ by indicating whether the child has been abandoned or whether one or both of the parents are dead. ## ὀρχέομαι (id: 003645000000000)

ὀρχέομαι
========

ὀρχέομαι (G3738)

verb

15\.244 · Linear Movement · Dance

to dance, dancing patterned rhythmic movements of the whole and/or parts of the body, normally to the accompaniment of music In a number of languages there are highly specific terms for various types of dancing, and one must make certain that the appropriate expression is used. Some terms for dancing may refer only to erotic dances; others, to highly ritualized performances in drama or religious practice; and still others may refer to personal expressions of happiness, joy, or grief. Special care must be exercised in selecting a term which will fit the context.

[MAT 11:17](https://ref.ly/Matt11:17) · [MAT 14:6](https://ref.ly/Matt14:6) · [MRK 6:22](https://ref.ly/Matt6:22) · [LUK 7:32](https://ref.ly/Mark7:32)

1:3](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:3)

89\.106 · Relations · Equivalence

**Collocation:** ὅ ἐστιν

that is, that means a marker of an explanation or a clarification in the same or a different language In some languages one must make more specific the interpretation of a term in another language. For example, in [MRK 3:17](https://ref.ly/Matt3:17) it may be necessary to translate ‘Boanerges, which in another language means Men of Thunder’ or ‘Boanerges, for which we would say, Men of Thunder.’

67\.36 · Time · A Point of Time with Reference to Other Points of Time: Before, Long Ago, Now, At the Same Time, When, About, After

**Collocation:** ὁσάκις ἐάν

whenever, as often as indefinite and multiple points of time, simultaneous with other corresponding points of time

[1CO 11:25](https://ref.ly/Rom11:25) · [1CO 11:26](https://ref.ly/Rom11:26) · [REV 11:6](https://ref.ly/Jude11:6)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Corinthians 11:25; 1 Corinthians 11:26; Revelation 11:6


## ὅσιος (id: 003648000000000)

ὅσιος
=====

ὅσιος (G3741)

adjc. \| \-α, \-ον \| ἀνόσιος · ὁσιότης · ὁσίως

88\.24 · Moral and Ethical Qualities and Related Behavior · Holy, Pure

holy, pure, divine pertaining to being holy in the sense of superior moral qualities and possessing certain essentially divine qualities in contrast with what is human1

[ACT 13:35](https://ref.ly/John13:35) · [TIT 1:8](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:8) · [HEB 7:26](https://ref.ly/Phlm7:26) · [REV 15:4](https://ref.ly/Jude15:4) · [REV 16:5](https://ref.ly/Jude16:5)

53\.46 · Religious Activities · Dedicate, Consecrate

devout, godly, dedicated pertaining to being dedicated or consecrated to the service of God In some languages it is essential to distinguish between ἅγιος and ὅσιος when these refer to God (see [88\.24](#88.24)) and when they refer to persons or objects which have been consecrated to the service of God. It is both theoretically and practically advantageous to make a significant distinction in meaning on the basis of these contextual differences which occur in the Greek NT. It is not difficult to translate ἅγιος and ὅσιος by expressions meaning ‘dedicated to God,’ ‘devout,’ ‘pious,’ etc., but it may be extremely difficult to translate ἅγιος and ὅσιος when applied to God. In some languages one can only use an expression such as ‘he who is truly God’ or ‘he who is in every respect God.’

[ACT 2:27](https://ref.ly/John2:27) · [1TI 2:8](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:8)

33\.290 · Communication · Promise

**Collocation:** τὰ ὅσια

divine promises, promises from God the divine matters which have been promised It is also possible to understand τὰ ὅσια in [ACT 13:34](https://ref.ly/John13:34) as meaning ‘divine decrees’ or ‘decrees made by God.’

[ACT 13:34](https://ref.ly/John13:34)

1 There may be certain subtle distinctions in the two sets of meanings in [88\.24](#88.24), those with the stem ἁγ\- and those with the stem ὁσ\-, but this cannot be determined with any degree of certainty from existing contexts.

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 13:35; Titus 1:8; Hebrews 7:26; Revelation 15:4; Revelation 16:5; Acts 2:27; 1 Timothy 2:8; Acts 13:34

holiness, divine quality the quality of holiness as an expression of the divine in contrast with the human For another interpretation of ὁσιότης in [LUK 1:75](https://ref.ly/Mark1:75), see [53\.45](#53.45).

[MAT 7:12](https://ref.ly/Matt7:12) · [MAT 13:44](https://ref.ly/Matt13:44) · [MAT 13:46](https://ref.ly/Matt13:46) · [MAT 17:12](https://ref.ly/Matt17:12) · [MAT 18:18](https://ref.ly/Matt18:18) · [MAT 18:18](https://ref.ly/Matt18:18) · [MAT 18:25](https://ref.ly/Matt18:25) · [MAT 25:40](https://ref.ly/Matt25:40) · [MAT 25:45](https://ref.ly/Matt25:45) · [MAT 28:20](https://ref.ly/Matt28:20) · [MRK 3:28](https://ref.ly/Matt3:28) · [MRK 9:13](https://ref.ly/Matt9:13) · [LUK 18:12](https://ref.ly/Mark18:12) · [JHN 6:11](https://ref.ly/Luke6:11) · [JHN 11:22](https://ref.ly/Luke11:22) · [JHN 16:13](https://ref.ly/Luke16:13) · [JHN 16:15](https://ref.ly/Luke16:15) · [ACT 3:22](https://ref.ly/John3:22) · [ACT 4:28](https://ref.ly/John4:28) · [ACT 9:16](https://ref.ly/John9:16) · [ROM 7:1](https://ref.ly/Acts7:1) · [2TI 1:18](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:18) · [HEB 3:3](https://ref.ly/Phlm3:3) · [REV 21:16](https://ref.ly/Jude21:16)

78\.52 · Degree · Up To, As Much As, To the Degree That \[Marked Extent of Degree]

to the degree that, to the same degree, as much as a degree of correlative extent Expressions involving correspondence of degrees must often be rather considerably restructured. For example, in the first part of [REV 18:7](https://ref.ly/Jude18:7) one may translate in some languages ‘she honored herself very much and lived in great luxury, and so give her very much suffering and grief.’ In the case of [HEB 1:4](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:4), one may translate ‘God gave his Son a name that was more important than the name of the angels; therefore, God’s Son is greater than the angels.’  
One may also render this expression in [HEB 8:6](https://ref.ly/Phlm8:6) as ‘Christ’s ministry is better than the old to the same degree that the covenant he mediates is better.’  
In [ROM 11:13](https://ref.ly/Acts11:13) ὅσον is strengthened by ἐπί (see [78\.51](#78.51)).

[MRK 7:36](https://ref.ly/Matt7:36) · [ROM 11:13](https://ref.ly/Acts11:13) · [HEB 1:4](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:4) · [HEB 3:3](https://ref.ly/Phlm3:3) · [HEB 7:20](https://ref.ly/Phlm7:20) · [HEB 8:6](https://ref.ly/Phlm8:6) · [HEB 9:27](https://ref.ly/Phlm9:27) · [HEB 10:25](https://ref.ly/Phlm10:25) · [REV 2:24](https://ref.ly/Jude2:24) · [REV 18:7](https://ref.ly/Jude18:7)

67\.139 · Time · Duration of Time with Reference to Some Unit of Time: During, In, While, Throughout

as long as, while an extent of time of the same length as another extent or unit of time The additon of ἐπί (degree, [78\.51](#78.51)) and/or χρόνος (time, [67\.78](#67.78)) reinforces this meaning of ὅσος.

[MAT 9:15](https://ref.ly/Matt9:15) · [MRK 2:19](https://ref.ly/Matt2:19) · [1CO 7:39](https://ref.ly/Rom7:39) · [GAL 4:1](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:1) · [1TI 6:1](https://ref.ly/2Thess6:1) · [HEB 10:37](https://ref.ly/Phlm10:37) · [HEB 10:37](https://ref.ly/Phlm10:37) · [2PE 1:13](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:13)

## ὀσφῦς (id: 003657000000000)

ὀσφῦς
=====

ὀσφῦς (G3751) — ὀσφύς

noun, f. \| \-ύος

8\.42 · Body, Body Parts, and Body Products · Parts of the Body

waist the part of the human body above the hips and below the ribs, the customary place for tying a belt In a number of languages the equivalent of ‘waist’ is a term meaning literally ‘the middle.’

[MAT 3:4](https://ref.ly/Matt3:4) · [MRK 1:6](https://ref.ly/Matt1:6)

8\.43 · Body, Body Parts, and Body Products · Parts of the Body

genitals, loins the male genital organs (‘loins’ in traditional English translations) A literal rendering of ‘he was still in the genitals of his father’ ([HEB 7:10](https://ref.ly/Phlm7:10)) might be regarded in a number of languages as being excessively specific and even vulgar. It is possible to render this passage as simply ‘he was not as yet born’ or ‘his father had not yet caused him to be conceived.’ In [ACT 2:30](https://ref.ly/John2:30) (καρποῦ τῆς ὀσφύος αὐτοῦ ‘the fruit of his loins’) it may be best to translate ‘one of his descendants’ (see [10\.34](#10.34)).

[ACT 2:30](https://ref.ly/John2:30) · [HEB 7:5](https://ref.ly/Phlm7:5) · [HEB 7:10](https://ref.ly/Phlm7:10)

77\.5 · Ready, Prepared

**Collocation:** περιζώννυμαι τὴν ὀσφύν

to get ready, to prepare oneself (an idiom, literally: to gird up the loins) to cause oneself to be in a state of readiness In the context of [EPH 6:14](https://ref.ly/Gal6:14), however, it may be useful to preserve the extended figure of readiness for warfare beginning with [EPH 6:13](https://ref.ly/Gal6:13) and extending through verse 17\.

[LUK 12:35](https://ref.ly/Mark12:35) · [EPH 6:14](https://ref.ly/Gal6:14) · [1PE 1:13](https://ref.ly/Jas1:13)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 3:4; Mark 1:6; Acts 2:30; Hebrews 7:5; Hebrews 7:10; Luke 12:35; Ephesians 6:14; 1 Peter 1:13


## ὅταν (id: 003658000000000)

ὅταν
====

ὅταν (G3752)

conj. \| ὅς \+ ἄν

67\.31 · Time · A Point of Time with Reference to Other Points of Time: Before, Long Ago, Now, At the Same Time, When, About, After

whenever, when an indefinite point or points of time, which may be roughly simultaneous to or overlap with another point of time (ὅταν\[a] and ἐπάν may also imply some degree of uncertainty)

[MAT 5:11](https://ref.ly/Matt5:11) · [MAT 6:2](https://ref.ly/Matt6:2) · [MAT 6:5](https://ref.ly/Matt6:5) · [MAT 6:6](https://ref.ly/Matt6:6) · [MAT 6:16](https://ref.ly/Matt6:16) · [MAT 10:19](https://ref.ly/Matt10:19) · [MAT 10:23](https://ref.ly/Matt10:23) · [MAT 13:32](https://ref.ly/Matt13:32) · [MAT 15:2](https://ref.ly/Matt15:2) · [MAT 23:15](https://ref.ly/Matt23:15) · [MAT 24:15](https://ref.ly/Matt24:15) · [MRK 4:31](https://ref.ly/Matt4:31) · [MRK 11:25](https://ref.ly/Matt11:25) · [MRK 13:11](https://ref.ly/Matt13:11) · [MRK 13:28](https://ref.ly/Matt13:28) · [MRK 14:7](https://ref.ly/Matt14:7) · [LUK 6:22](https://ref.ly/Mark6:22) · [LUK 6:22](https://ref.ly/Mark6:22) · [LUK 6:26](https://ref.ly/Mark6:26) · [LUK 11:2](https://ref.ly/Mark11:2) · [LUK 12:11](https://ref.ly/Mark12:11) · [LUK 12:54](https://ref.ly/Mark12:54) · [LUK 12:55](https://ref.ly/Mark12:55) · [LUK 14:8](https://ref.ly/Mark14:8) · [LUK 14:12](https://ref.ly/Mark14:12) · [LUK 14:13](https://ref.ly/Mark14:13) · [LUK 21:30](https://ref.ly/Mark21:30) · [JHN 7:27](https://ref.ly/Luke7:27) · [JHN 7:31](https://ref.ly/Luke7:31) · [JHN 16:13](https://ref.ly/Luke16:13) · [ACT 24:22](https://ref.ly/John24:22) · [ROM 2:14](https://ref.ly/Acts2:14) · [1CO 3:4](https://ref.ly/Rom3:4) · [1CO 14:26](https://ref.ly/Rom14:26) · [1CO 16:12](https://ref.ly/Rom16:12) · [2CO 10:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:6) · [2CO 12:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:10) · [COL 4:16](https://ref.ly/Phil4:16) · [1JN 5:2](https://ref.ly/2Pet5:2)

67\.30 · Time · A Point of Time with Reference to Other Points of Time: Before, Long Ago, Now, At the Same Time, When, About, After

when a point of time which is roughly simultaneous to or overlaps with another point of time

[REV 8:1](https://ref.ly/Jude8:1)1

67\.36 · Time · A Point of Time with Reference to Other Points of Time: Before, Long Ago, Now, At the Same Time, When, About, After

whenever, as often as indefinite and multiple points of time, simultaneous with other corresponding points of time

[MRK 3:11](https://ref.ly/Matt3:11) · [JHN 8:44](https://ref.ly/Luke8:44) · [JHN 10:4](https://ref.ly/Luke10:4) · [2CO 13:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:9)

67\.139 · Time · Duration of Time with Reference to Some Unit of Time: During, In, While, Throughout

as long as, while an extent of time of the same length as another extent or unit of time

[JHN 9:5](https://ref.ly/Luke9:5)

1 REV 8:1 It is possible that ὅταν occurs in [REV 8:1](https://ref.ly/Jude8:1) because of the prophetic nature of the context, so that in reality, though the verb is in the past tense, the reference is ultimately to a future event which is indefinite in time. If this is the case, then ὅταν\[b] may simply be classified with ὅταν\[a] ([67\.31](#67.31)).

## ὅτε (id: 003659000000000)

ὅτε
===

ὅτε (G3753)

conj. \| ὅς \+ τέ

67\.30 · Time · A Point of Time with Reference to Other Points of Time: Before, Long Ago, Now, At the Same Time, When, About, After

when a point of time which is roughly simultaneous to or overlaps with another point of time

[MAT 7:28](https://ref.ly/Matt7:28)

as long as, while an extent of time of the same length as another extent or unit of time

[MRK 15:41](https://ref.ly/Matt15:41) · [JHN 17:12](https://ref.ly/Luke17:12) · [ROM 6:20](https://ref.ly/Acts6:20) · [ROM 7:5](https://ref.ly/Acts7:5) · [GAL 4:3](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:3)

## ὅτι (id: 003660000000000)

ὅτι
===

ὅτι (G3754)

conj. \| ὅς \+ τίς

90\.21 · Case · Content

that, the fact that a marker of discourse content, whether direct or indirect The marking of direct and indirect discourse varies greatly from language to language. In some instances there is no marker at all; in other cases one marker precedes and another follows. In still other instances a repetition of an expression of speaking occurs at the end of a quotation, whether direct or indirect. In a number of languages written usage differs appreciably from spoken usage, because intonation so often marks introduced discourse in the spoken form of a language.

89\.33 · Relations · Cause and/or Reason

69\.13 · Affirmation, Negation · Markers for an Affirmative Response to Questions

**Collocation:** οὐ μή

not a marker of a strongly emphatic affirmative response to a question

[JHN 11:56](https://ref.ly/Luke11:56) · [JHN 18:11](https://ref.ly/Luke18:11)

1 οὐ\[a] and μή\[a] are lexically equivalent in meaning, but they have a syntactic distribution which may be said to give them certain distinctions in so\-called class meaning. However, since these differences are based upon syntactic distributions, such distinctions in meaning are beyond the scope of this type of lexical analysis. The form οὐ, of course, tends to occur primarily in indicative clauses, while μή occurs generally in non\-indicative clauses. 2 MAT 27:13 In a number of languages, however, an affirmative response to such a question would be indicated by a negative particle, so that the answer to the question in [MAT 27:13](https://ref.ly/Matt27:13) would be ‘no.’ 3 The combination οὐ μή\[d] may be regarded technically as a type of idiom in that the two negations do not cancel each other out, but constitute an emphatic negation.

## οὗ (id: 003662000000000)

οὗ
==

οὗ (G3757)

advb. \| ὅς

83\.5 · Spacial Positions · Where, Somewhere, Everywhere

where, wherever a reference to a position in space (often used with ἐάν or ἄν ‘ever,’ [71\.8](#71.8), to mark an indefinite and unrestricted position in space)

[MAT 2:9](https://ref.ly/Matt2:9) · [MAT 18:20](https://ref.ly/Matt18:20) · [MAT 28:16](https://ref.ly/Matt28:16) · [LUK 4:16](https://ref.ly/Mark4:16) · [LUK 4:17](https://ref.ly/Mark4:17) · [LUK 10:1](https://ref.ly/Mark10:1) · [LUK 23:53](https://ref.ly/Mark23:53) · [LUK 24:28](https://ref.ly/Mark24:28) · [ACT 1:13](https://ref.ly/John1:13) · [ACT 2:2](https://ref.ly/John2:2) · [ACT 7:29](https://ref.ly/John7:29) · [ACT 12:12](https://ref.ly/John12:12) · [ACT 16:13](https://ref.ly/John16:13) · [ACT 20:8](https://ref.ly/John20:8) · [ACT 25:10](https://ref.ly/John25:10) · [ACT 28:14](https://ref.ly/John28:14) · [ROM 9:26](https://ref.ly/Acts9:26) · [1CO 16:6](https://ref.ly/Rom16:6) · [COL 3:1](https://ref.ly/Phil3:1) · [HEB 3:9](https://ref.ly/Phlm3:9)

92\.28 · Discourse Referentials · Relative Reference

where, in which a relative reference to a set of circumstances

[ROM 4:15](https://ref.ly/Acts4:15) · [ROM 5:20](https://ref.ly/Acts5:20) · [2CO 3:17](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:17) · [HEB 3:9](https://ref.ly/Phlm3:9) · [REV 17:15](https://ref.ly/Jude17:15)

## οὐά (id: 003663000000000)

οὐά
===

οὐά (G3758)

intj.

33\.411 · Communication · Mock, Ridicule

aha an exclamation of mocking and ridicule Almost all languages have interjections or exclamatory expressions indicating ridicule. If such does not exist in a language, one can always translate this expression in [MRK 15:29](https://ref.ly/Matt15:29) as ‘now what about you, you who are going to destroy the sanctuary and in three days build it up again!’

[MRK 15:29](https://ref.ly/Matt15:29)

* **Associated Passages:** Mark 15:29


## οὐαί (id: 003664000000000)

οὐαί
====

οὐαί (G3759)

intj.

22\.9 · Trouble, Hardship, Relief, Favorable Circumstances · Trouble, Hardship, Distress

disaster, horror a state of intense hardship or distress In some languages there may not be a noun for ‘disaster,’ but one can express the meaning of the Greek term οὐαί as ‘how greatly one will suffer’ or ‘what terrible pain will come to one.’

advb. \| οὐ \+ δέ \+ ποτέ

never, not ever, at no time an indefinite negated point of time

91\.7 · Discourse Markers · Markers of Emphasis

surely, indeed, then a marker of somewhat greater emphasis (in comparison with μέν\[b], γέ, and δή, [91\.6](#91.6))

89\.127 · Relations · Contrast

but a marker of relatively weak contrast In a context such as [JHN 9:18](https://ref.ly/Luke9:18), ὅτι may also serve as a type of transition.

## οὔπω (id: 003673000000000)

οὔπω
====

οὔπω (G3768)

advb. \| οὐ \+ \-πω

67\.129 · Time · Duration of Time with Reference to Some Point of Time: Until, Delay, Still, From

not yet, still not the negation of extending time up to and beyond an expected point

## οὐρά (id: 003674000000000)

οὐρά
====

οὐρά (G3769)

noun, f. \| \-ᾶς

8\.44 · Body, Body Parts, and Body Products · Parts of the Body

tail tail of an animal or insect In some languages it may be important to distinguish clearly between the tail of a mammal, a fish, and a creature such as the scorpion.

[REV 9:10](https://ref.ly/Jude9:10) · [REV 9:10](https://ref.ly/Jude9:10) · [REV 9:19](https://ref.ly/Jude9:19) · [REV 9:19](https://ref.ly/Jude9:19) · [REV 12:4](https://ref.ly/Jude12:4)

* **Associated Passages:** Revelation 9:10; Revelation 9:19; Revelation 12:4


## οὐράνιος (id: 003675000000000)

οὐράνιος
========

οὐράνιος (G3770)

adjc. \| \-ον \| οὐρανός

1\.12 · Geographical Objects and Features · Regions Above the Earth

heavenly, in heaven, pertaining to heaven (derivative of οὐρανός\[b] heaven, [1\.11](#1.11)) related to or located in heaven The derivatives οὐράνιος and ἐπουράνιος\[b] are essentially equivalent to semantic compounds in that they combine a semantic element of heaven as a celestial abode with another semantic element specifying location or relationship. Frequently such terms are translated as ‘in heaven’ or ‘from heaven’ or ‘belonging to heaven.’

[MAT 5:48](https://ref.ly/Matt5:48) · [MAT 6:14](https://ref.ly/Matt6:14) · [MAT 6:26](https://ref.ly/Matt6:26) · [MAT 6:32](https://ref.ly/Matt6:32) · [MAT 15:13](https://ref.ly/Matt15:13) · [MAT 18:35](https://ref.ly/Matt18:35) · [MAT 23:9](https://ref.ly/Matt23:9) · [ACT 26:19](https://ref.ly/John26:19)

12\.30 · Supernatural Beings and Powers · Supernatural Beings

**Collocation:** στρατιὰ οὐράνιος

throng of angels, ranks of angels (an idiom, literally: heavenly army) a large group or throng of angels There may be distinct objections to speaking of στρατιὰ οὐράνιος as being a ‘heavenly army,’ though undoubtedly in earlier times the thought of angels being God’s military force would have been quite appropriate. Therefore, in place of some expression which may refer to an army or military unit, it may be possible to translate στρατιὰ οὐράνιος as simply ‘many, many angels’ or ‘row after row of angels.’

[LUK 2:13](https://ref.ly/Mark2:13)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 5:48; Matthew 6:14; Matthew 6:26; Matthew 6:32; Matthew 15:13; Matthew 18:35; Matthew 23:9; Acts 26:19; Luke 2:13


## οὐρανόθεν (id: 003676000000000)

οὐρανόθεν
=========

οὐρανόθεν (G3771)

advb. \| οὐρανός

1\.9 · Geographical Objects and Features · Regions Above the Earth

from the sky the sky as a source or as a location from which implied movement takes place οὐρανόθεν in [ACT 14:17](https://ref.ly/John14:17) and [ACT 26:13](https://ref.ly/John26:13) is somewhat ambiguous, for both events may be regarded as involving a semantic derivative of οὐρανός\[b] heaven ([1\.11](#1.11)) rather than οὐρανός\[a] sky ([1\.5](#1.5)). In [ACT 14:17](https://ref.ly/John14:17) one might very well translate ‘he gives rain from heaven,’ since obviously the agent is God. Similarly, in [ACT 26:13](https://ref.ly/John26:13) the light might come from heaven, since the light is presumably regarded as a supernatural phenomenon.

[ACT 14:17](https://ref.ly/John14:17) · [ACT 26:13](https://ref.ly/John26:13)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 14:17; Acts 26:13


## οὐρανός (id: 003677000000000)

οὐρανός
=======

οὐρανός (G3772)

noun, m. \| \-οῦ \| ἐπουράνιος · μεσουράνημα · οὐράνιος · οὐρανόθεν

1\.5 · Geographical Objects and Features · Regions Above the Earth

sky space above the earth, including the vault arching high over the earth from one horizon to another, as well as the sun, moon, and stars In [ACT 2:5](https://ref.ly/John2:5) the expression ‘under the sky’ is better translated in a number of languages as ‘on earth,’ and in [HEB 11:12](https://ref.ly/Phlm11:12) ‘the stars of the sky’ is effectively rendered in many instances as ‘the stars up above,’ though in many languages ‘up above’ is not only redundant, but misleading, since it might suggest stars that would not be ‘up above.’ The area described by the phrase ἀπ’ ἄκρων οὐρανῶν ἕως τῶν ἄκρων αὐτῶν in [MAT 24:31](https://ref.ly/Matt24:31) refers to the limits of the horizon regarded as the limits of the sky, but in many languages the equivalent is an expression dealing with the limits of the earth.  
In some contexts οὐρανός\[a] ‘sky’ designates areas which in other languages are referred to by terms specifying only a part of the area above the earth. For example, a literal translation of ἐμβλέψατε εἰς τὰ πετεινὰ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ ‘look at the birds of the sky’ ([MAT 6:26](https://ref.ly/Matt6:26)) would in some languages refer only to those birds which fly particularly high in the sky, for example, eagles, vultures, and falcons. The Greek expression τὰ πετεινὰ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ simply designates wild birds in contrast with domestic fowl, such as chickens. Therefore, in translating ‘the birds of the sky,’ one may wish to use a general designation for all wild birds. See also [4\.41](#4.41).  
The sky (οὐρανός\[a]) is also represented in the Scriptures as a dwelling place of certain supernatural beings, for the various stars and constellations were associated with supernatural forces (see στρατιὰ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ, literally ‘the army of heaven,’ in [ACT 7:42](https://ref.ly/John7:42): ὁ θεὸς ... παρέδωκεν αὐτοὺς λατρεύειν τῇ στρατιᾷ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ ‘God ... gave them over to worship the stars of the heaven’). In some languages, however, there is a problem with the rendering ‘stars of heaven,’ since such an expression might imply that there are types of stars which do not exist in the sky. Therefore, it is often more appropriate to translate στρατιὰ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ as simply ‘the stars.’ At the same time it is often advisable to introduce a marginal note to indicate that the stars were regarded as symbols of supernatural beings. One may also translate στρατιὰ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ as ‘supernatural beings in the sky’ or ‘powers that dwell in the sky’ (see [12\.45](#12.45)).  
The semantic equivalent of οὐρανός\[a] is in some instances merely an adverb meaning ‘up’ or ‘above.’ In other languages the various areas referred to by ούρανός\[a] must be designated by more specific terms or phrases, for example, ‘the region of the stars,’ ‘the place of the clouds,’ or ‘where the wind blows,’ as a way of designating at least three different areas which may be significant in certain types of contexts.

3:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:7) · [2PE 3:10](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:10) · [2PE 3:12](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:12) · [2PE 3:13](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:13) · [REV 5:13](https://ref.ly/Jude5:13) · [REV 10:6](https://ref.ly/Jude10:6) · [REV 11:6](https://ref.ly/Jude11:6) · [REV 12:1](https://ref.ly/Jude12:1) · [REV 12:3](https://ref.ly/Jude12:3) · [REV 12:4](https://ref.ly/Jude12:4) · [REV 15:1](https://ref.ly/Jude15:1) · [REV 16:21](https://ref.ly/Jude16:21) · [REV 21:1](https://ref.ly/Jude21:1) · [REV 21:1](https://ref.ly/Jude21:1)

1\.11 · Geographical Objects and Features · Regions Above the Earth

heaven the supernatural dwelling place of God and other heavenly beings (οὐρανός\[b] also contains a component denoting that which is above or in the sky, but the element of abode is evidently more significant than location above the earth) In a number of languages precisely the same term is used to designate both ‘sky’ and ‘heaven’ (as the abode of God). But in many instances a completely separate term must be employed in speaking of the dwelling place of God, for example, ‘where God lives’ or ‘where God is’ or ‘from where God governs.’ In some languages the term referring to ‘heaven’ is simply ‘the home above,’ and in one instance a designation of heaven refers primarily to a state, for example, ‘the life above.’  
The phrase τρίτος οὐρανός ‘the third heaven’ is a fixed phrase referring to the abode of God (ἁρπαγέντα τὸν τοιοῦτον ἕως τρίτου οὐρανοῦ ‘(a man) who was caught up as far as the third heaven’ [2CO 12:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:2)), but in a number of languages it is extremely difficult to speak of ‘the third heaven,’ especially if one uses a term for ‘heaven’ which is ‘the abode of God.’ Therefore, in many instances τρίτος οὐρανός is translated as ‘heaven’ or ‘the abode of God,’ and if necessary, a footnote may be added in order to indicate the literal meaning of the Greek expression. If a literal rendering is employed, it is usually equivalent to ‘the third sky’ or ‘above two degrees of sky.’

21:2](https://ref.ly/Jude21:2) · [REV 21:10](https://ref.ly/Jude21:10)

12\.16 · Supernatural Beings and Powers · Supernatural Beings

God (a figurative extension of meaning of οὐρανός\[b] ‘heaven,’ [1\.11](#1.11)) a reference to God based on the Jewish tendency to avoid using a name or direct term for God A translation such as ‘I have sinned against God’ would seem to be so much simpler than ‘I have sinned against heaven,’ but in reality it is not easy to find a satisfactory expression for ‘sinning against God.’ It is, of course, possible to speak of ‘fighting against a person,’ but ‘to sin against a person’ involves a very complex relationship which often needs to be more clearly explained, for example, ‘I have sinned against what God has said’ or ‘I have sinned by doing what displeases God.’ In some languages a strictly literal rendering of ‘I have sinned against God’ might be understood in the sense of committing some kind of sin with God.

[MAT 3:2](https://ref.ly/Matt3:2) · [MAT 4:17](https://ref.ly/Matt4:17) · [MAT 5:3](https://ref.ly/Matt5:3) · [MAT 5:10](https://ref.ly/Matt5:10) · [MAT 5:19](https://ref.ly/Matt5:19) · [MAT 5:19](https://ref.ly/Matt5:19) · [MAT 5:20](https://ref.ly/Matt5:20) · [MAT 7:21](https://ref.ly/Matt7:21) · [MAT 8:11](https://ref.ly/Matt8:11) · [MAT 10:7](https://ref.ly/Matt10:7) · [MAT 11:11](https://ref.ly/Matt11:11) · [MAT 11:12](https://ref.ly/Matt11:12) · [MAT 13:11](https://ref.ly/Matt13:11) · [MAT 13:24](https://ref.ly/Matt13:24) · [MAT 13:31](https://ref.ly/Matt13:31) · [MAT 13:33](https://ref.ly/Matt13:33) · [MAT 13:44](https://ref.ly/Matt13:44) · [MAT 13:45](https://ref.ly/Matt13:45) · [MAT 13:47](https://ref.ly/Matt13:47) · [MAT 13:52](https://ref.ly/Matt13:52) · [MAT 16:1](https://ref.ly/Matt16:1) · [MAT 16:17](https://ref.ly/Matt16:17) · [MAT 16:19](https://ref.ly/Matt16:19) · [MAT 16:19](https://ref.ly/Matt16:19) · [MAT 16:19](https://ref.ly/Matt16:19) · [MAT 18:1](https://ref.ly/Matt18:1) · [MAT 18:3](https://ref.ly/Matt18:3) · [MAT 18:4](https://ref.ly/Matt18:4) · [MAT 18:23](https://ref.ly/Matt18:23) · [MAT 19:12](https://ref.ly/Matt19:12) · [MAT 19:14](https://ref.ly/Matt19:14) · [MAT 19:23](https://ref.ly/Matt19:23) · [MAT 20:1](https://ref.ly/Matt20:1) · [MAT 21:25](https://ref.ly/Matt21:25) · [MAT 21:25](https://ref.ly/Matt21:25) · [MAT 22:2](https://ref.ly/Matt22:2) · [MAT 23:13](https://ref.ly/Matt23:13) · [MAT 23:22](https://ref.ly/Matt23:22) · [MAT 25:1](https://ref.ly/Matt25:1) · [LUK 15:18](https://ref.ly/Mark15:18) · [LUK 15:21](https://ref.ly/Mark15:21) · [JHN 3:27](https://ref.ly/Luke3:27) · [2CO 5:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:2)

1\.3 · Geographical Objects and Features · Universe, Creation

**Collocation:** ὁ οὐρανὸς καὶ ἡ γῆ

heaven and earth, universe a more or less fixed phrase: the heavens and the earth, equivalent to a single lexical item: the totality of God’s creation There may be certain complications involved in rendering ὁ οὐρανὸς καὶ ἡ γῆ as ‘heaven and earth,’ since ‘heaven’ might be interpreted in some languages as referring only to the dwelling place of God himself. The referents in this passage are ‘the sky and the earth,’ in other words, all of physical existence, but not the dwelling place of God, for the latter would not be included in what is destined to pass away.

[MAT 5:18](https://ref.ly/Matt5:18) · [MAT 11:25](https://ref.ly/Matt11:25) · [MAT 24:35](https://ref.ly/Matt24:35) · [MRK 13:31](https://ref.ly/Matt13:31) · [LUK 10:21](https://ref.ly/Mark10:21) · [LUK 16:17](https://ref.ly/Mark16:17) · [LUK 21:33](https://ref.ly/Mark21:33) · [ACT 4:24](https://ref.ly/John4:24) · [REV 14:7](https://ref.ly/Jude14:7) · [REV 20:11](https://ref.ly/Jude20:11)

4\.41 · Animals · Birds

**Collocation:** πετεινὰ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ

birds, wild birds (an idiom, literally: birds of the sky) wild birds in contrast with domesticated birds such as chickens A literal rendering of πετεινὰ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ, either as ‘birds of the air’ or ‘birds of the sky,’ may be misleading, since ‘birds of the air’ might imply that these are birds made out of air, and ‘birds of the sky’ might suggest they are birds which remain always in the sky. In some languages the equivalent expression is ‘birds of the trees’ or ‘birds of the winds.’

[MAT 6:26](https://ref.ly/Matt6:26) · [MAT 8:20](https://ref.ly/Matt8:20) · [MAT 13:32](https://ref.ly/Matt13:32) · [MRK 4:32](https://ref.ly/Matt4:32) · [LUK 8:5](https://ref.ly/Mark8:5) · [LUK 9:58](https://ref.ly/Mark9:58) · [LUK 13:19](https://ref.ly/Mark13:19) · [ACT 10:12](https://ref.ly/John10:12) · [ACT 11:6](https://ref.ly/John11:6)

12\.45 · Supernatural Beings and Powers · Supernatural Powers

**Collocation:** στρατιὰ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ

the stars of heaven (an idiom, literally: army of heaven) the stars of heaven as symbols of various supernatural powers Since ‘the stars of heaven’ serve primarily as symbols of certain heavenly supernatural powers, it may be appropriate in some languages to speak of ‘the spirits of the stars in the sky.’ See also [1\.5](#1.5).

[ACT 7:42](https://ref.ly/John7:42)

1\.43 · Geographical Objects and Features · The Earth's Surface

**Collocation:** ὑπὸ τὸν οὐρανόν

on earth (an idiom, literally: under the sky) pertaining to being on the earth ὑπὸ τὸν οὐρανόν is essentially equivalent in reference to ἐπίγειος ‘on the earth’ ([1\.41](#1.41)).

[ACT 2:5](https://ref.ly/John2:5) · [ACT 4:12](https://ref.ly/John4:12) · [COL 1:23](https://ref.ly/Phil1:23)

## οὖς (id: 003680000000000)

οὖς
===

οὖς (G3775)

noun, n. \| \-ὠτός \| ἐνωτίζομαι · ὠτάριον · ὠτίον

8\.24 · Body, Body Parts, and Body Products · Parts of the Body

ear{N:001}

[MAT 11:15](https://ref.ly/Matt11:15) · [MAT 13:9](https://ref.ly/Matt13:9) · [MAT 13:15](https://ref.ly/Matt13:15) · [MAT 13:16](https://ref.ly/Matt13:16) · [MAT 13:43](https://ref.ly/Matt13:43) · [MRK 4:9](https://ref.ly/Matt4:9) · [MRK 4:23](https://ref.ly/Matt4:23) · [MRK 7:33](https://ref.ly/Matt7:33) · [MRK 8:18](https://ref.ly/Matt8:18) · [LUK 8:8](https://ref.ly/Mark8:8) · [LUK 14:35](https://ref.ly/Mark14:35) · [LUK 22:50](https://ref.ly/Mark22:50) · [ACT 7:51](https://ref.ly/John7:51) · [ROM 11:8](https://ref.ly/Acts11:8) · [1CO 2:9](https://ref.ly/Rom2:9) · [1CO 12:16](https://ref.ly/Rom12:16) · [REV 2:7](https://ref.ly/Jude2:7) · [REV 2:11](https://ref.ly/Jude2:11) · [REV 2:17](https://ref.ly/Jude2:17) · [REV 2:29](https://ref.ly/Jude2:29) · [REV 3:6](https://ref.ly/Jude3:6) · [REV 3:13](https://ref.ly/Jude3:13) · [REV 3:22](https://ref.ly/Jude3:22) · [REV 13:9](https://ref.ly/Jude13:9)

24\.54 · Sensory Events and States · Hear

to hear, hearing (a figurative extension of meaning of οὖς\[a] ‘ear,’ [8\.24](#8.24)) the activity of hearing

[LUK 1:44](https://ref.ly/Mark1:44) · [LUK 4:21](https://ref.ly/Mark4:21) · [ACT 11:22](https://ref.ly/John11:22) · [ACT 28:27](https://ref.ly/John28:27) · [JAS 5:4](https://ref.ly/Heb5:4) · [1PE 3:12](https://ref.ly/Jas3:12)

28\.73 · Know · Not Able To Be Known, Secret

**Collocation:** εἰς τὸ οὖς · πρὸς τὸ οὖς

in secret, in private, privately (idioms, literally: into the ear, and: to the ear) pertaining to what is known only by those who hear a particular message in private A phrase such as ‘whatever you hear in secret’ may be rendered as ‘whatever you hear when no one else is listening.’  
For another treatment of the phrase πρὸς τὸ οὖς λαλέω, see [33\.91](#33.91).

[MAT 10:27](https://ref.ly/Matt10:27) · [LUK 12:3](https://ref.ly/Mark12:3)

33\.91 · Communication · Speak, Talk

**Collocation:** πρὸς τὸ οὖς λαλέω

to whisper, to speak quietly (an idiom, literally: to speak to the ear) to speak quietly and presumably to a restricted audience For another treatment of the phrase πρὸς τὸ οὖς, see [28\.73](#28.73).

[LUK 12:3](https://ref.ly/Mark12:3)

24\.70 · Sensory Events and States · Hear

**Collocation:** συνέχω τὰ ὦτα

to refuse to listen, to refuse to pay attention (an idiom, literally: hold the ears closed) to refuse to listen to what is being said In some languages the refusal to hear may be expressed idiomatically as ‘they put their hands over their ears’ or ‘they closed their ears’ or ‘the words never entered their heads.’

[ACT 7:57](https://ref.ly/John7:57)

24\.64 · Sensory Events and States · Hear

**Collocation:** τίθεμαι εἰς τὰ ὦτα

to listen carefully to (an idiom, literally: to put into the ears) to listen carefully to, with the implication of not forgetting For another interpretation of this idiom in [LUK 9:44](https://ref.ly/Mark9:44), see [29\.5](#29.5).

[LUK 9:44](https://ref.ly/Mark9:44)

29\.5 · Memory and Recall · Storing of Information

**Collocation:** τίθεμαι εἰς τὰ ὦτα

to bear in mind, to remember well, to not forget (an idiom, literally: to place in the ears) to continue to bear something in mind For another interpretation of the idiom τίθεμαι εἰς τὰ ὦτα in [LUK 9:44](https://ref.ly/Mark9:44), see [24\.64](#24.64).

[LUK 9:44](https://ref.ly/Mark9:44)

32\.46 · Understand · Lack of Capacity for Understanding

**Collocation:** τοῖς ὠσὶν βαρέως ἀκούω

to be slow to understand, to be mentally dull (an idiom, literally: to hear heavily with the ears) to be mentally slow or dull in comprehending

[MAT 13:15](https://ref.ly/Matt13:15) · [ACT 28:27](https://ref.ly/John28:27)

1 ὠτίον and ὠτσᾇριον are formally diminutive derivatives of οὖς, but how much diminutive meaning is involved is questionable.

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 11:15; Matthew 13:9; Matthew 13:15; Matthew 13:16; Matthew 13:43; Mark 4:9; Mark 4:23; Mark 7:33; Mark 8:18; Luke 8:8; Luke 14:35; Luke 22:50; Acts 7:51; Romans 11:8; 1 Corinthians 2:9; 1 Corinthians 12:16; Revelation 2:7; Revelation 2:11; Revelation 2:17; Revelation 2:29; Revelation 3:6; Revelation 3:13; Revelation 3:22; Revelation 13:9; Luke 1:44; Luke 4:21; Acts 11:22; Acts 28:27; James 5:4; 1 Peter 3:12; Matthew 10:27; Luke 12:3; Acts 7:57; Luke 9:44


## οὐσία (id: 003681000000000)

οὐσία
=====

οὐσία (G3776)

noun, f. \| \-ας \| εἰμί

57\.19 · Possess, Transfer, Exchange · Have, Possess, Property, Owner

property, wealth (derivative of εἰμί\[c] ‘to exist,’ [13\.69](#13.69)) that which exists as property and wealth In most contexts in which οὐσία occurs in non\-biblical Greek, the reference is to considerable possessions or wealth, and accordingly it would be appropriate in [LUK 15:12](https://ref.ly/Mark15:12) to speak of ‘estate.’

[LUK 15:12](https://ref.ly/Mark15:12) · [LUK 15:13](https://ref.ly/Mark15:13)

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 15:12; Luke 15:13

89\.106 · Relations · Equivalence

**Collocation:** τοῦτ᾽ ἔστιν

that is, that means a marker of an explanation or a clarification in the same or a different language

[MAT 27:46](https://ref.ly/Matt27:46) · [MRK 7:2](https://ref.ly/Matt7:2) · [ACT 1:19](https://ref.ly/John1:19) · [ACT 2:12](https://ref.ly/John2:12) · [ACT 19:4](https://ref.ly/John19:4) · [ROM 1:12](https://ref.ly/Acts1:12) · [ROM 7:18](https://ref.ly/Acts7:18) · [ROM 9:8](https://ref.ly/Acts9:8) · [ROM 10:6](https://ref.ly/Acts10:6) · [ROM 10:7](https://ref.ly/Acts10:7) · [ROM 10:8](https://ref.ly/Acts10:8)

1 The reference in the discourse may be to that which precedes or follows. 2 The pejorative meaning of οὗτος depends primarily upon the total context and not upon οὗτος as such.

## ὀφειλέτης (id: 003686000000000)

ὀφειλέτης
=========

ὀφειλέτης (G3781)

noun, m. \| \-ου \| ὀφείλω

57\.222 · Possess, Transfer, Exchange · Owe, Debt, Cancel

debtor a person who is in debt

[MAT 18:24](https://ref.ly/Matt18:24)

71\.27 · Mode · Should, Ought

one who is obliged to, one who is obligated to, one who must (derivative of ὀφείλω\[c] ‘to be under obligation,’ [71\.25](#71.25)) one who is obligated to do something

[ROM 1:14](https://ref.ly/Acts1:14) · [ROM 8:12](https://ref.ly/Acts8:12) · [ROM 15:27](https://ref.ly/Acts15:27) · [GAL 5:3](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:3)

88\.300 · Moral and Ethical Qualities and Related Behavior · Sin, Wrongdoing, Guilt

sinner, offender (derivative of ὀφείλω\[d] ‘to sin against,’ [88\.298](#88.298)) one who commits sin and thus incurs a moral debt

[MAT 6:12](https://ref.ly/Matt6:12) · [LUK 13:4](https://ref.ly/Mark13:4)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 18:24; Romans 1:14; Romans 8:12; Romans 15:27; Galatians 5:3; Matthew 6:12; Luke 13:4


## ὀφειλή (id: 003687000000000)

ὀφειλή
======

ὀφειλή (G3782)

noun, f. \| \-ῆς \| ὀφείλω

57\.221 · Possess, Transfer, Exchange · Owe, Debt, Cancel

debt, amount owed (derivative of ὀφείλω\[a] ‘to owe,’ [57\.219](#57.219)) that which is owed

[MAT 18:32](https://ref.ly/Matt18:32) · [ROM 13:7](https://ref.ly/Acts13:7)

71\.24 · Mode · Should, Ought

what one should do, duty that which ought to be done as a matter of duty or social obligation

[1CO 7:3](https://ref.ly/Rom7:3)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 18:32; Romans 13:7; 1 Corinthians 7:3


## ὀφείλημα (id: 003688000000000)

ὀφείλημα
========

ὀφείλημα (G3783)

noun, n. \| \-τος \| ὀφείλω

57\.221 · Possess, Transfer, Exchange · Owe, Debt, Cancel

debt, amount owed (derivative of ὀφείλω\[a] ‘to owe,’ [57\.219](#57.219)) that which is owed It is also possible to understand ὀφείλημα in [ROM 4:4](https://ref.ly/Acts4:4) as meaning ‘obligation’ (see [71\.26](#71.26)).

[ROM 4:4](https://ref.ly/Acts4:4)

71\.26 · Mode · Should, Ought

obligation that which one must do out of a sense of duty or as the result of commitment For another interpretation of ὀφείλημα in [ROM 4:4](https://ref.ly/Acts4:4), see [57\.221](#57.221).

[ROM 4:4](https://ref.ly/Acts4:4)

88\.299 · Moral and Ethical Qualities and Related Behavior · Sin, Wrongdoing, Guilt

offense, sin, transgression, guilt (derivative of ὀφείλω\[d] ‘to sin against,’ [88\.298](#88.298)) the moral debt incurred as the result of sin

[MAT 6:12](https://ref.ly/Matt6:12)1

1 MAT 6:12 Some scholars interpret ὀφείλημα in [MAT 6:12](https://ref.ly/Matt6:12) as referring literally to debts, since they believe the statement refers to the actual forgiveness of debts in the idealized jubilee year in the Jewish Law.

* **Associated Passages:** Romans 4:4; Matthew 6:12

## ὄφελον (id: 003690000000000)

ὄφελον
======

ὄφελον (G3785)

part. \| ὀφείλω

71\.28 · Mode · Should, Ought

would that that which ought to be if one only had one’s wish In a number of languages it may be preferable to translate ὄφελον as simply an expression of wishing. Therefore, [2CO 11:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:1) might be rendered as ‘I wish you would grant me a bit of foolishness’ or ‘I wish you would tolerate a bit of foolishness on my part.’

[1CO 4:8](https://ref.ly/Rom4:8) · [2CO 11:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:1) · [GAL 5:12](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:12) · [REV 3:15](https://ref.ly/Jude3:15)

## ὄφελος (id: 003691000000000)

ὄφελος
======

ὄφελος (G3786)

noun, n. \| \-ους \| ὠφελέω

65\.40 · Value · Advantageous, Not Advantageous

advantage, benefit, beneficial pertaining to a benefit to be derived from some ob ject, event, or state In a number of languages the equivalent of ‘benefit’ or ‘beneficial’ is often ‘that which helps.’ Accordingly, in [1TI 4:8](https://ref.ly/2Thess4:8) one may render ἡ γὰρ σωματικὴ γυμνασία πρὸς ὀλίγον ἐστὶν ὠφέλιμος as ‘physical exercise helps to a small extent’ or ‘if one exercises one’s body, that helps a little.’

[1CO 15:32](https://ref.ly/Rom15:32) · [JAS 2:14](https://ref.ly/Heb2:14) · [JAS 2:16](https://ref.ly/Heb2:16)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Corinthians 15:32; James 2:14; James 2:16

## ὀφθαλμός (id: 003693000000000)

ὀφθαλμός
========

ὀφθαλμός (G3788)

noun, m. \| \-οῦ \| ἀντοφθαλμέω · μονόφθαλμος · ὁράω · ὀφθαλμοδουλία

8\.23 · Body, Body Parts, and Body Products · Parts of the Body

eye eye, normally including the eyelids In a number of languages an important distinction is made between the eyes consisting of the eyeballs and the eyes covered or partially covered by the eyelids. In [MAT 9:29](https://ref.ly/Matt9:29) and [MAT 20:34](https://ref.ly/Matt20:34), Jesus apparently touched the eyelids, not the eyeballs.

4:8](https://ref.ly/Jude4:8) · [REV 5:6](https://ref.ly/Jude5:6) · [REV 7:17](https://ref.ly/Jude7:17) · [REV 19:12](https://ref.ly/Jude19:12) · [REV 21:4](https://ref.ly/Jude21:4)

24\.16 · Sensory Events and States · See

seeing, sight (a figurative extension of meaning of ὀφθαλμός\[a] ‘eye,’ [8\.23](#8.23)) the capacity to see

[MAT 21:42](https://ref.ly/Matt21:42) · [MRK 12:11](https://ref.ly/Matt12:11) · [ACT 1:9](https://ref.ly/John1:9) · [1CO 12:16](https://ref.ly/Rom12:16) · [1CO 12:17](https://ref.ly/Rom12:17)1 · [1CO 12:21](https://ref.ly/Rom12:21) · [GAL 3:1](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:1) · [HEB 4:13](https://ref.ly/Phlm4:13) · [1PE 3:12](https://ref.ly/Jas3:12) · [1JN 2:16](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:16) · [REV 1:7](https://ref.ly/Jude1:7)

32\.24 · Understand · Capacity for Understanding

to be able to understand, to come to perceive, understanding (a figurative extension of meaning of ὀφθαλμός\[a] ‘eye,’ [8\.23](#8.23)) capacity to understand as the result of perception In both [LUK 19:42](https://ref.ly/Mark19:42) and [EPH 1:18](https://ref.ly/Gal1:18) ὀφθαλμός designates a faculty of understanding and is not a reference merely to perception. In [EPH 1:18](https://ref.ly/Gal1:18) καρδία denotes the mind (see [26\.3](#26.3)), so that the phrase ‘the eyes of the mind’ really means ‘the capacity for understanding which the mind has.’

[LUK 19:42](https://ref.ly/Mark19:42) · [JHN 12:40](https://ref.ly/Luke12:40) · [ACT 26:18](https://ref.ly/John26:18) · [ACT 28:27](https://ref.ly/John28:27) · [ROM 3:18](https://ref.ly/Acts3:18) · [EPH 1:18](https://ref.ly/Gal1:18)

88\.165 · Moral and Ethical Qualities and Related Behavior · Envy, Jealousy

**Collocation:** ὀφθαλμὸς πονηρός

jealous(y) (an idiom, literally: evil eye) a feeling of jealousy and resentment because of what someone else has or does It is also possible to understand ὀφθαλμὸς πονηρός in [MAT 20:15](https://ref.ly/Matt20:15) as meaning ‘stinginess’ (see [57\.108](#57.108)). See also [23\.149](#23.149).

[MAT 20:15](https://ref.ly/Matt20:15) · [MRK 7:22](https://ref.ly/Matt7:22)

57\.108 · Possess, Transfer, Exchange · Give

**Collocation:** ὀφθαλμὸς πονηρός

stingy, miserly (an idiom, literally: evil eye) to be stingy It is also possible that ὀφθαλμὸς πονηρός in [MAT 20:15](https://ref.ly/Matt20:15) is to be understood in the sense of ‘jealous’ (see [88\.165](#88.165)). See also [23\.149](#23.149).

[MAT 6:23](https://ref.ly/Matt6:23) · [MAT 20:15](https://ref.ly/Matt20:15) · [MRK 7:22](https://ref.ly/Matt7:22)

23\.69 · Physiological Processes and States · Sleep, Waking

**Collocation:** ἦσαν οἱ ὀφθαλμοὶ βεβαρημένοι · ἦσαν οἱ ὀφθαλμοὶ καταβαρυνόμενοι

to have become very sleepy, to be very sleepy (idioms, literally: their eyes were weighed down) to become excessively or exceedingly sleepy In some languages the equivalent of ἦσαν οἱ ὀφθαλμοὶ βεβαρημένοι (or καταβαρυνόμενοι) would be ‘they could no longer keep their eyes open’ or ‘they could not drive sleep away.’

[MAT 26:43](https://ref.ly/Matt26:43) · [MRK 14:40](https://ref.ly/Matt14:40)

27\.50 · Learn · Be Willing To Learn

**Collocation:** καμμύω τοὺς ὀφθαλμούς

to refuse to learn, to refuse to recognize (an idiom, literally: to close the eyes) to be unwilling to learn and to evaluate something fairly In a number of languages there is a problem involved in a literal rendering of ‘to close the eyes,’ since this may be understood in a strictly literal sense or as denoting death.

[MAT 13:15](https://ref.ly/Matt13:15) · [ACT 28:27](https://ref.ly/John28:27)

32\.25 · Understand · Capacity for Understanding

**Collocation:** τυφλόω τοὺς ὀφθαλμούς

to cause to not understand, to make unable to comprehend (an idiom, literally: to blind the eyes) to cause someone to no longer have the capacity for understanding Though in some languages there is a close relationship between the eyes and comprehension, in many languages it is simply impossible to use the idiom ‘to blind the eyes’ in speaking of ‘causing someone not to understand.’ The problem becomes particularly difficult in speaking of ‘darkness blinding the eyes’ as in [1JN 2:11](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:11). People can understand how ‘bright light would blind the eyes,’ but it would be strange indeed to think of ‘darkness’ having such an effect. In the context of [1JN 2:11](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:11), the darkness is, of course, not literal darkness but a state of sinfulness. Therefore, in some languages it may be necessary to translate ‘their sinfulness has made it impossible for them to understand’ or ‘because they sin, they cannot understand.’

[JHN 12:40](https://ref.ly/Luke12:40) · [1JN 2:11](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:11)

1 1CO 12:17 It would, of course, be possible to interpret ὀφθαλμός in [1CO 12:17](https://ref.ly/Rom12:17) as meaning simply ‘eye,’ and therefore one might translate ‘as if the whole body were an eye.’ But the following terms such as ἀκοή ([24\.53](#24.53)) and ὄσφρησις ([24\.71](#24.71)) indicate quite clearly that it is the sensory faculty or capacity which is involved.

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 5:29; Matthew 5:38; Matthew 6:22; Matthew 7:3; Matthew 7:4; Matthew 7:5; Matthew 9:29; Matthew 9:30; Matthew 13:15; Matthew 13:16; Matthew 17:8; Matthew 18:9; Matthew 20:33; Mark 8:18; Mark 8:25; Mark 9:47; Luke 2:30; Luke 4:20; Luke 6:20; Luke 6:41; Luke 6:42; Luke 10:23; Luke 11:34; Luke 16:23; Luke 18:13; Luke 24:16; Luke 24:31; John 4:35; John 6:5; John 9:6; John 9:10; John 9:11; John 9:14; John 9:15; John 9:17; John 9:21; John 9:26; John 9:30; John 9:32; John 10:21; John 11:37; John 11:41; John 17:1; Acts 9:8; Acts 9:18; Acts 9:40; Romans 11:8; Romans 11:10; 1 Corinthians 2:9; 1 Corinthians 15:52; Galatians 4:15; 2 Peter 2:14; 1 John 1:1; 1 John 2:11; Revelation 1:14; Revelation 2:18; Revelation 3:18; Revelation 4:6; Revelation 4:8; Revelation 5:6; Revelation 7:17; Revelation 19:12; Revelation 21:4; Matthew 21:42; Mark 12:11; Acts 1:9; 1 Corinthians 12:16; 1 Corinthians 12:17; 1 Corinthians 12:21; Galatians 3:1; Hebrews 4:13; 1 Peter 3:12; 1 John 2:16; Revelation 1:7; Luke 19:42; John 12:40; Acts 26:18; Acts 28:27; Romans 3:18; Ephesians 1:18; Matthew 20:15; Mark 7:22; Matthew 6:23; Matthew 26:43; Mark 14:40


## ὄφις (id: 003694000000000)

ὄφις
====

ὄφις (G3789)

noun, m. \| \-εως

4\.52 · Animals · Reptiles and Other 'Creeping Things'

snake, reptile long legless reptile; no eyelids or external ears; moves by wriggling or gliding; some species have a poisonous bite; symbol of lurking, unexpected danger; associated with lies, deception, and misleading teaching Though ὄφις\[a] may refer to any kind of snake, in [LUK 10:19](https://ref.ly/Mark10:19) it is obviously a reference to a poisonous snake that would harm a person who stepped on it. In translating [LUK 10:19](https://ref.ly/Mark10:19) it is essential to avoid an expression which will mean merely ‘to walk on snakes’ as though one were to walk for some distance over a mass of swarming snakes. The implication of this context is simply that one is able to step on snakes without being harmed.

[MAT 7:10](https://ref.ly/Matt7:10) · [MAT 10:16](https://ref.ly/Matt10:16) · [MRK 16:18](https://ref.ly/Matt16:18) · [LUK 10:19](https://ref.ly/Mark10:19) · [LUK 11:11](https://ref.ly/Mark11:11) · [JHN 3:14](https://ref.ly/Luke3:14) · [1CO 10:9](https://ref.ly/Rom10:9) · [2CO 11:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:3) · [REV 9:19](https://ref.ly/Jude9:19) · [REV 12:9](https://ref.ly/Jude12:9) · [REV 12:14](https://ref.ly/Jude12:14) · [REV 12:15](https://ref.ly/Jude12:15) · [REV 20:2](https://ref.ly/Jude20:2)

88\.123 · Moral and Ethical Qualities and Related Behavior · Bad, Evil, Harmful, Damaging

evil person, viper, snake (a figurative extension of meaning of ὄφις\[a] snake, [4\.52](#4.52)) a dangerous and despised person One must exercise due caution in rendering ἔχιδνα in [MAT 12:34](https://ref.ly/Matt12:34) and ὄφις in [MAT 23:33](https://ref.ly/Matt23:33), since in some cultures snakes are regarded as being particularly wise and clever, and therefore an expression such as ‘brood of vipers’ could be a compliment. In some languages important distinctions are made between different types of snakes, and therefore connotations may vary considerably.

[MAT 23:33](https://ref.ly/Matt23:33)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 7:10; Matthew 10:16; Mark 16:18; Luke 10:19; Luke 11:11; John 3:14; 1 Corinthians 10:9; 2 Corinthians 11:3; Revelation 9:19; Revelation 12:9; Revelation 12:14; Revelation 12:15; Revelation 20:2; Matthew 23:33

ὀφρῦς
=====

ὀφρῦς (G3790) — ὀφρύς

noun, f. \| \-ύος

1\.49 · Geographical Objects and Features · Elevated Land Formations

cliff, brow of a hill (a figurative extension of meaning of ὀφρῦς ‘brow,’ not occurring in the NT) the upper portion of a precipitous or steep contour of a hill or mountain

## ὀχλέω (id: 003696000000000)

ὀχλέω
=====

ὀχλέω (G3791)

verb \| ἐνοχλέω · ὀχλοποιέω · ὄχλος · παρενοχλέω

22\.24 · Trouble, Hardship, Relief, Favorable Circumstances · Cause Trouble, Hardship

to afflict, to cause suffering to cause hardship by continual annoyance

## ὀχλοποιέω (id: 003697000000000)

ὀχλοποιέω
=========

ὀχλοποιέω (G3792)

verb \| ὀχλέω \+ ποιέω

11\.2 · Groups and Classes of Persons and Members of Such Groups and Classes · General

to gather a crowd or to cause a crowd to form to cause a crowd to gather The context of [ACT 17:5](https://ref.ly/John17:5) indicates clearly that the crowd was an unruly and undisciplined gathering of people, and therefore one may often employ an expression equivalent to ‘they gathered together a mob.’

[ACT 17:5](https://ref.ly/John17:5)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 17:5


## ὄχλος (id: 003698000000000)

ὄχλος
=====

ὄχλος (G3793)

noun, m. \| \-ου \| ὀχλέω

11\.1 · Groups and Classes of Persons and Members of Such Groups and Classes · General

crowd, multitude a casual non\-membership group of people, fairly large in size and assembled for whatever purpose1 In a number of languages there is no term corresponding to ‘crowd.’ One may, however, usually speak of such a group of people as ‘many people’ or ‘many men and women.’ Depending on the context, it may besary to indicate some relative difference in size, for example, ‘very large crowd’ or ‘many, many people.’ If the crowd is relatively small, one may sometimes speak of ‘just some people.’

19:1](https://ref.ly/Jude19:1) · [REV 19:6](https://ref.ly/Jude19:6)

87\.64 · Status · Low Status or Rank \[including persons of low status]

common people, rabble the common people, in contrast with those who are rich, leaders, and/or authorities in the society, often with the implication of disdain and low esteem In a number of languages the equivalent of ‘the common people’ is ‘the poor people,’ but in some languages an equivalent depends upon geographical location, for example, ‘those who live on the edge of town’ (the opposite situation from what exists in most present\-day metropolitan centers). In still other languages ‘the common people’ may be designated in terms of lack of status, for example, ‘those who have no position’ or ‘those whom the rich do not greet’ or ‘those before whom no one ever bows.’

[MAT 14:5](https://ref.ly/Matt14:5) · [MAT 21:26](https://ref.ly/Matt21:26) · [MAT 21:46](https://ref.ly/Matt21:46) · [LUK 13:14](https://ref.ly/Mark13:14) · [LUK 23:4](https://ref.ly/Mark23:4) · [JHN 7:49](https://ref.ly/Luke7:49)

1 In this set of terms referring to a crowd or a multitude, it is possible that πλῆθος\[b] denotes a somewhat larger group of persons than the other terms and that λαός\[c] suggests a crowd made up of common people, but one cannot be certain of these distinctions from existing contexts.

## ὀχύρωμα (id: 003699000000000)

ὀχύρωμα
=======

ὀχύρωμα (G3794)

noun, n. \| \-τος

7\.21 · Constructions · Buildings

stronghold, fortress a strong military fortification The term ὀχύρωμα occurs in the NT only in [2CO 10:4](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:4), where it is used figuratively of the strength of false arguments. In a number of languages it may be more satisfactory to render ὀχύρωμα as a kind of simile so as to mark this figurative usage, for example, ‘powerful weapons with which to destroy false arguments in the same way that people would destroy fortresses.’ In some languages a ‘fortress’ may be described in terms of its function, for example, ‘a place for protection’ or ‘a place to defend oneself.’ Often, however, a fortress is described in terms of its construction, for example, ‘a strong\-walled place.’

[2CO 10:4](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:4)

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Corinthians 10:4


## ὀψάριον (id: 003700000000000)

ὀψάριον
=======

ὀψάριον (G3795)

noun, n. \| \-ου \| ὀψώνιον · παροψίς

5\.16 · Foods and Condiments · Food

fish fish intended for food or the flesh of fish as food In Greek the usual meaning of ὀψάριον is a tidbit of food eaten with bread, but it occurs in later Greek in the meaning of fish, and in the NT this meaning occurs only in the fourth Gospel (compare the usage in [JHN 6:9](https://ref.ly/Luke6:9) with ἰχθύς in parallel passages).  
Many languages distinguish clearly between terms for fish while still living in the water and fish which have been caught and presumably are to be used for food.

[JHN 6:9](https://ref.ly/Luke6:9) · [JHN 6:11](https://ref.ly/Luke6:11) · [JHN 21:9](https://ref.ly/Luke21:9) · [JHN 21:10](https://ref.ly/Luke21:10) · [JHN 21:13](https://ref.ly/Luke21:13)

* **Associated Passages:** John 6:9; John 6:11; John 21:9; John 21:10; John 21:13


## ὀψέ (id: 003701000000000)

ὀψέ
===

ὀψέ (G3796)

prep. (with gen.), advb. \| ὄψιος

67\.76 · Time · A Point of Time with Reference to Units of Time: Daybreak, Midday, Midnight, Late

late, late in the day pertaining to a point near the end of a day (normally after sunset but before night) It is also possible to understand ὀψέ in [MRK 11:19](https://ref.ly/Matt11:19) as indicating a period of time, namely ‘evening’ (see [67\.197](#67.197)).

[MRK 11:19](https://ref.ly/Matt11:19)

67\.197 · Time · Definite Units of Time: Year, Month, Week, Day, Hour

evening the period after sunset and before darkness (compare [67\.191](#67.191))

[MRK 13:35](https://ref.ly/Matt13:35)

67\.51 · Time · A Point of Time with Reference to Other Points of Time: Before, Long Ago, Now, At the Same Time, When, About, After

after a point of time subsequent to another point of time, with the implication of relative lateness

[MAT 28:1](https://ref.ly/Matt28:1)

* **Associated Passages:** Mark 11:19; Mark 13:35; Matthew 28:1


## ὄψιμος (id: 003702000000000)

ὄψιμος
======

ὄψιμος (G3797)

adjc. \| \-ον \| ὄψιος

14\.13 · Physical Events and States · Rain

late rain, spring rain (derivative of ὄψιος ‘late,’ [67\.76](#67.76), and used absolutely of ‘late rain’) a rain that comes late in the season, that is to say, in April or May after the normal rains of the winter season have passed See πρόϊμος ‘early rain’ ([14\.14](#14.14)) for a discussion of problems involving ‘late rain’ and ‘early rain.’

[JAS 5:7](https://ref.ly/Heb5:7)

* **Associated Passages:** James 5:7

ὄψιος
=====

ὄψιος (G3798) — ὀψία

adjc. \| \-α, \-ον \| ὀψέ · ὀψία · ὄψιμος

67\.76 · Time · A Point of Time with Reference to Units of Time: Daybreak, Midday, Midnight, Late

late, late in the day pertaining to a point near the end of a day (normally after sunset but before night)

[JHN 20:19](https://ref.ly/Luke20:19)

67\.197 · Time · Definite Units of Time: Year, Month, Week, Day, Hour

**Collocation:** ὀψία

evening the period after sunset and before darkness (compare [67\.191](#67.191))

ὄψις
====

ὄψις (G3799)

noun, f. \| \-εως \| ὁράω

8\.18 · Body, Body Parts, and Body Products · Parts of the Body

face the front part of the human head

[JHN 11:44](https://ref.ly/Luke11:44) · [REV 1:16](https://ref.ly/Jude1:16)

30\.14 · Think · To Think, Thought

outward appearance, external form that which is thought to be true but is not necessarily so, since it is based upon mere appearance and external form

## ὀψώνιον (id: 003705000000000)

ὀψώνιον
=======

ὀψώνιον (G3800)

noun, n. \| \-ου \| ὀψάριον \+ ὠνέομαι

57\.166 · Possess, Transfer, Exchange · Pay, Price, Cost

pay, compensation the payment made to soldiers as ration money

[LUK 3:14](https://ref.ly/Mark3:14) · [1CO 9:7](https://ref.ly/Rom9:7)

57\.118 · Possess, Transfer, Exchange · Give

money for support, money to live on money which is needed for living expenses ὀψώνιον in [2CO 11:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:8) may be rendered as ‘money on which to live’ or ‘money for food and lodging.’

[2CO 11:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:8)

89\.42 · Relations · Result

wages, result (a figurative extension of meaning of ὀψώνιον\[a] ‘a soldier’s wages,’ [57\.166](#57.166)) the end result from some activity, viewed as something which one receives in return

[ROM 6:23](https://ref.ly/Acts6:23)

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 3:14; 1 Corinthians 9:7; 2 Corinthians 11:8; Romans 6:23
